campbell.title: Campbell City Council — February 3, 2026
campbell.summary: The Campbell City Council met to approve a $4,000 donation from Water Tower Kitchen to support youth scholarships in the recreation program. They also discussed plans to build a bicycle and pedestrian bridge over Highway 17 at Hamilton Avenue, including approving studies and submitting grant applications to help fund the project.
keyTopics: Water Tower Kitchen donates $4,000 for youth recreation scholarships
New bicycle and pedestrian bridge planned over Highway 17 at Hamilton Avenue
City submitting grant applications to fund the Hamilton Avenue crossing project
Extension of interim ordinance #2326 under consideration

saratoga.title: Saratoga City Council — March 18, 2026
saratoga.summary: The City Council held a public hearing on March 18, 2026 to get community input on a new rule or policy. After hearing from residents, the council moved forward by introducing an ordinance (new city rule) and skipped the usual first-reading step to speed up the process. The ordinance will be voted on for final approval at the next council meeting.
keyTopics: Public hearing held to gather resident feedback
New ordinance introduced and fast-tracked for adoption
Final vote scheduled for next regular council meeting
First reading requirement waived to expedite the process

los-gatos.title: Los Gatos City Council — April 7, 2026
los-gatos.summary: The Los Gatos City Council met on April 7, 2026, to approve meeting minutes from previous sessions held in March and to authorize the Town Manager to sign a construction contract with QLM, Inc. The agenda was primarily focused on administrative housekeeping and moving forward with a construction project.
keyTopics: Approved minutes from six previous meetings (March 16, 17, and 30)
Authorized construction contract with QLM, Inc.
Town Manager given authority to execute the construction agreement

san-jose.title: San José City Council — April 21, 2026
san-jose.summary: San José City Council approved several service agreements and planning updates on April 21, 2026. The council dealt with airport baggage system maintenance, affordable housing protections, storm drain system planning, and a family camp project, along with routine committee business.
keyTopics: Airport baggage conveyor system contract renewal with Vanderlande Industries
Protections for affordable housing if subsidy programs end or properties are foreclosed
Master plan update for the city's storm sewer system
FEMA San José Family Camp project decisions

mountain-view.title: Mountain View City Council — April 14, 2026
mountain-view.summary: The Mountain View City Council held a meeting to review the city's spending plans for the upcoming fiscal year, focusing on major projects and improvements the city wants to fund. They also formally recognized Teen Month and National Volunteer Week to celebrate young people and volunteers in the community.
keyTopics: 2026-27 budget for capital projects (buildings, infrastructure, etc.)
Teen Month proclamation
National Volunteer Week proclamation

sunnyvale.title: Sunnyvale City Council — April 7, 2026
sunnyvale.summary: The Sunnyvale City Council held their regular meeting on April 7, 2026, where they recognized Arab-American Heritage Month and Fair Housing Month. The council opened with a flag salute and highlighted these two important observances as part of their special order of business.
keyTopics: Arab-American Heritage Month recognition
Fair Housing Month observance
Regular City Council meeting proceedings

cupertino.title: Cupertino City Council — April 13, 2026
cupertino.summary: The city council held a workshop to help residents understand and use OpenGov, a free online tool that shows how Cupertino spends its money and manages its budget. The council also reminded people that if you're paid to influence city decisions, you need to register as a lobbyist and report your activities.
keyTopics: OpenGov financial transparency tool demo and public access
City budget information now available online for residents
Lobbyist registration requirements for paid advocates
How to report lobbying activities to the city

santa-clara.title: Santa Clara City Council — April 16, 2026
santa-clara.summary: The Santa Clara City Council meeting on April 16, 2026 included important legal notices about filing lawsuits or challenges to city decisions. Residents have 90 days from the date a decision becomes final to file any legal challenge, unless a different deadline is specified elsewhere.
keyTopics: 90-day deadline to sue or challenge city decisions
Legal disclosure requirements for quasi-adjudicative decisions
California Code of Civil Procedure Section 1094.6 guidelines

milpitas.title: Milpitas City Council — April 7, 2026
milpitas.summary: The Milpitas City Council met on April 7, 2026 to handle several administrative matters, including reviewing and approving a plan update that affects how federal housing and community development funding is used in the city. The council also discussed potential changes to how council vacancies are filled and reviewed various meeting schedules and agendas.
keyTopics: Update to the city's 2025-2030 plan for federal housing and development funds
Potential changes to the process for filling vacant City Council seats
Review of council members' requests for new items to work on this year
Approval of previous meeting minutes and scheduling of upcoming meetings

palo-alto.title: Palo Alto City Council — April 9, 2026
palo-alto.summary: The Palo Alto City Council held a public hearing about a building at 405 Kipling Street in downtown. The owner is asking to upgrade the building's historic status and get permission to add 5,000 extra square feet of floor space as a reward for preserving the historic building and making it earthquake-safe.
keyTopics: 405 Kipling Street historic building upgrade request
5,000 square feet bonus floor area for seismic improvements
Historic Resources Board meeting schedule updates
Downtown commercial zone development incentives
